summary:
Current Summary:

Bell rings. Alex storms into class, classmates' snickers echoing.

"Look what we have here... Cripple!"

Alex glares, fists clenched. "Shut up, all of you."

Laughter ensues. The professor calls out names, with Alex's last.

"Alex, show us your pathetic magic."

Classmates jeer as Alex steps forward, heart racing.

"Go on, show us something useless," they mock.

Alex closes his eyes and focuses. Silence. Nothing happens again.

The professor frowns. "This is unacceptable. Last chance, Alex."

More mockery. Alex's pride burns.

"Pathetic! Just as weak as last year!"

Isolation sinks in. Alex turns, fleeing to an empty table.

Inside head: Weak. Stupid. Useless.

Next class starts soon.

Dining hall clears. Alex grabs a plate and ignores others.

Voice whispers, "Take mine."

Alex looks up to see a small girl.

"No thanks," he says bitterly.

The girl shrinks and lowers her head.

Professor enters, eyes on Alex.

"Alex, show us something... or leave."

More mockery. Alex's name is called again.

Silence. Nothing happens...

New Chapter Content:

Inside his head: Despair. Betrayal. Why should anyone care?

Dining hall clears. Alex grabs a plate and ignores others when a voice whispers, "Take mine." He looks up to see a small girl with tears in her eyes.

"No thanks," he says bitterly, feeling the weight of everyone's disdain.

The girl shrinks and lowers her head, but this time, she doesn't move away entirely. Instead, she whispers, "Please."

Alex hesitates for a moment before taking his meal from his own plate to hers. "Why?"

She looks up at him, eyes brimming with gratitude. "You're not weak," she says softly.

Inside Alex's head: Maybe... maybe there’s more than just mockery and isolation.

Professor enters the classroom, eyes on Alex.

"Alex, show us something... or leave."

More mockery. Alex's name is called again as he stands silently, feeling a flicker of hope amidst the despair.

Silence. Nothing happens...
